project.name: pixelforge
project.description: 8-Bit Avatar Generator — Generate retro 8-bit pixel avatars using LLMs (Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenAI-Compatible, DeepSeek). Fully client-side.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project README with setup instructions, UI layout diagram, tech stack listing, and usage guide for PixelForge 8-Bit Avatar Generator

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entry point with inline theme initialization script, Press Start 2P font import, and module script reference to /src/main.tsx

nodes.name: opencode.json
nodes.summary: OpenCode configuration enabling the Context7 MCP server for remote documentation queries

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: NPM package manifest defining scripts, React 18 dependencies, and Vite/Vitest/TypeScript dev toolchain

nodes.name: tsconfig.app.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript configuration for the app source with ES2020 target, react-jsx, strict mode, bundler module resolution, and @/* path alias to ./src/*

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Root TypeScript project references file pointing to tsconfig.app.json and tsconfig.node.json

nodes.name: tsconfig.node.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript configuration for Node/Vite config files with ES2022 target and bundler module resolution

nodes.name: 1-1-project-scaffold-and-build-tooling.md
nodes.summary: Story spec for scaffolding the 8bit-ai project with Vite 6 + React 18 + TypeScript strict mode, single-file build via vite-plugin-singlefile, @/ path alias, directory structure (components/hooks/services/types/utils), Vitest + React Testing Library setup, and starter template cleanup

nodes.name: 1-2-design-system-and-theme-toggle.md
nodes.summary: Story spec for NES-inspired design system with CSS custom properties for light (parchment) and dark (navy CRT) themes, Press Start 2P Google Fonts, useTheme hook with localStorage persistence, ThemeToggle component with 44px touch targets, scanline overlay via body::after pseudo-element, and focus-visible accessibility

nodes.name: 1-3-api-key-input-and-provider-configuration.md
nodes.summary: Story spec for API key input with show/hide password toggle and sessionStorage persistence, custom ProviderSelect dropdown with keyboard navigation supporting 4 providers (Anthropic/OpenAI/OpenAI-Compatible/DeepSeek), provider presets with auto-fill API URL and model, config persistence to localStorage on successful generate, and BYOK security rules

nodes.name: 1-4-layout-shell-control-panel-and-viewer-shell.md
nodes.summary: Story spec for app layout with PIXELFORGE header, three numbered sections (01 CONFIG/02 PROMPT/03 AVATAR) with 3px borders, ControlPanel with textarea/FORGE button/Cmd+Enter shortcut/6 example chips (Wizard/Knight/Robot/Astronaut/Ninja/Elf), ViewerShell empty state with pixel icon, and responsive design at 600px and 360px breakpoints

nodes.name: 2-1-llm-provider-abstraction-layer.md
nodes.summary: Story spec for LLM provider abstraction layer using Strategy pattern with LLMProvider interface, 4 provider implementations (Anthropic Messages API + OpenAI/OpenAI-Compatible/DeepSeek Chat Completions), llmClient dispatch, Result<T,E> typed error handling for API errors and network failures, system prompt enforcing JSON-only 16x16 pixel map responses

nodes.name: 2-2-pixel-generation-flow-and-state-management.md
nodes.summary: Story spec for pixel generation flow with usePixelGeneration hook managing pixelMap/isGenerating/error/selectedType state, ControlPanel prefix type selection with highlighted chips, ViewerShell four-state transitions (empty→loading→result→error), ErrorDisplay component, and LLM prompt construction from selected type and character name

nodes.name: 2-3-avatar-rendering-loading-indicator-and-png-export.md
nodes.summary: Story spec for avatar CSS Grid rendering from pixel map data, LoadingIndicator with 48px accent blinking square and pulsing GENERATING text, client-side PNG export via OffscreenCanvas.convertToBlob, DownloadButton with pixelforce-[type]-[name].png filename, RE-FORGE button for regeneration, and ViewerShell result state with action buttons

nodes.name: deferred-work.md
nodes.summary: Tracks deferred work items from code review, currently noting cross-platform monospace font rendering differences for PNG export as a pre-existing issue

nodes.name: sprint-status.yaml
nodes.summary: Sprint 1 status tracking YAML for 8bit-ai project, documenting development status of epic-1 (done), epic-2 (review), individual story statuses from 1-1 through 2-3, feat-002 epics, and review history with patch counts for completed stories

nodes.name: architecture.md
nodes.summary: Architecture Decision Document for PixelForge — defines tech stack (Vite 6 + React 18 + TypeScript + vite-plugin-singlefile), project structure, component tree, LLM provider strategy pattern, Result<T,E> error handling, theme system, and implementation patterns with naming conventions and state flow rules.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown planning document with YAML frontmatter, code examples, and file tree

nodes.name: epics-FEAT-002.md
nodes.summary: Epic breakdown for FEAT-002 (HTML Download Export) — defines one epic with two stories: buildAvatarHTML pure function and HTML export utility with download button. Covers FR-1 through FR-7 for self-contained HTML export, including privacy (no API key leakage), portability (offline rendering), and NFR requirements.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown planning document with YAML frontmatter, requirement tables, and Gherkin-style acceptance criteria

nodes.name: epics.md
nodes.summary: Epic breakdown for FEAT-001 (main project) — two epics: Epic 1 (Project Foundation & User Setup) with stories 1.1-1.4 covering scaffold, design system, API key input, layout shell; Epic 2 (Avatar Generation, Rendering & Export) with stories 2.1-2.3 covering LLM provider abstraction, pixel generation flow, avatar rendering, loading indicator, and PNG export.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown planning document with YAML frontmatter, requirement inventories, coverage maps, and Gherkin-style acceptance criteria

nodes.name: ux-design-specification.md
nodes.summary: UX Design Specification for PixelForge — defines retro NES-inspired design system with CSS custom properties for dark/light themes, Press Start 2P typography, scanline overlay, pixel-loader animation, avatar viewer state machine (empty/loading/result/error), custom components (section containers, pixel buttons, chips, custom select), responsive breakpoints, accessibility targets (WCAG Level A), and user journey flows.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown UX specification with YAML frontmatter, colour system tables, Mermaid flowchart, and component inventory

nodes.name: feat-002-1-1-build-avatar-html.md
nodes.summary: Story spec for FEAT-002.1.1: buildAvatarHTML pure function that produces a self-contained HTML string from pixel map data for offline avatar export

nodes.name: feat-002-1-2-html-export-utility.md
nodes.summary: Story spec for FEAT-002.1.2: HTML export utility & Download HTML button, adding client-side .html download alongside existing PNG export

nodes.name: .decision-log.md
nodes.summary: Decision log for FEAT-002 PRD: hobby stakes, CSS Grid over table, no footer, fixed light background for export

nodes.name: prd.md
nodes.summary: PRD for FEAT-002 HTML Download Export: self-contained .html export of pixel avatars with API key safety, pure function buildAvatarHTML, and client-side download

nodes.name: .decision-log.md
nodes.summary: Decision log for FEAT-001 PRD: hobby stakes, fast path, multi-provider LLM support, theme persistence in localStorage

nodes.name: prd.md
nodes.summary: PRD for FEAT-001 PixelForge 8-Bit Avatar Generator: single-file app with BYOK LLM pixel generation, CSS grid rendering, PNG export, multi-provider support, and theme toggle

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore patterns for understand-anything analysis tool, with commented-out exclusions for .agents, .opencode, _bmad, docs, and test files

nodes.name: FEAT-001-requirement.md
nodes.summary: Requirement spec for FEAT-001: single-file HTML pixel avatar generator with LLM pixel map generation, API key handling, prefix types, and client-side PNG download

nodes.name: pixelforge-avatar-generator.html
nodes.summary: Reference design HTML file for PixelForge avatar generator with dark/light theme CSS variables, Press Start 2P font, scanline overlay, and full UI layout

nodes.name: FEAT-002-requirement.md
nodes.summary: Requirement spec for FEAT-002: Download HTML button, self-contained .html export with inline CSS, no API key leakage, pixel-accurate rendering

properties.summary: Defines and exports SYSTEM_PROMPT — the system instruction for LLM providers that enforces JSON output schema (palette, 16x16 grid of hex colors, label) with NES-inspired 64-color palette constraint.

properties.summary: Vitest test suite for DeepSeekProvider. Tests: successful request/response flow, default model fallback to 'deepseek-v4-flash', and network error handling returning NETWORK_ERROR code.

properties.summary: Implements LLMProvider for DeepSeek API. Sends POST to /v1/chat/completions with Bearer auth, system prompt, and user prompt. Handles API errors (non-2xx), empty responses, truncation (finish_reason=length), and network failures. Uses deepseek-v4-flash as default model.

nodes.name: src/services/DeepSeekProvider.ts

properties.summary: Vitest test suite for llmClient.generate(). Tests dispatching to all four provider types (anthropic, openai, openai-compatible, deepseek) and returns INVALID_PROVIDER error for unknown provider types.

properties.summary: Central LLM client with lazy singleton provider registry. Exports async generate() that dispatches PixelGenRequest to the correct LLMProvider by type string. Returns INVALID_PROVIDER error for unknown types.

nodes.summary: Node: file:src/services/OpenAIProvider.ts

nodes.name: src/services/parseResponse.test.ts
nodes.summary: Node: file:src/services/parseResponse.test.ts

properties.summary: Parses and validates raw JSON strings from LLM responses into PixelMapResponse. Validates: palette (non-empty array of 6-digit hex colors), grid (non-empty square array of hex colors), label (non-empty string). Returns Result type with PARSE_ERROR on failure.

nodes.name: src/services/parseResponse.ts
nodes.summary: Node: file:src/services/parseResponse.ts

properties.summary: Defines the LLMProvider interface — the contract for all AI provider implementations. Requires a single async generate() method taking PixelGenRequest and returning Result<PixelMapResponse>.

nodes.name: src/services/providers.ts
nodes.summary: Node: file:src/services/providers.ts

properties.summary: Test setup entry point for Vitest. Imports @testing-library/jest-dom to extend Vitest matchers with DOM-specific assertions.

nodes.name: src/test-setup.ts
nodes.summary: Node: file:src/test-setup.ts

properties.summary: Core type definitions for the pixel avatar system. Defines: LLMProviderType union, ProviderConfig, ProviderPreset with PROVIDER_PRESETS constant (Anthropic/OpenAI/OpenAI-Compatible/DeepSeek), PixelGenRequest (prompt, apiUrl, model, apiKey), PixelMapResponse (palette, grid, label), AppError, and tagged union Result type.

nodes.name: src/types/pixelmap.ts
nodes.summary: Node: file:src/types/pixelmap.ts

properties.summary: Comprehensive Vitest test suite for buildAvatarHTML. Tests: HTML5 doctype, html lang attribute, charset meta, viewport meta, title format, absence of scripts/external links/external images, single style block, determinism, no API key leakage, cell background rendering, no class for colors, grid-columns CSS, total cell count, label position after grid, 50KB size limit for 32x32, and display:grid layout.

nodes.name: src/utils/buildAvatarHTML.test.ts
nodes.summary: Node: file:src/utils/buildAvatarHTML.test.ts

properties.summary: Builds a self-contained HTML document for displaying a pixel avatar. Generates CSS grid layout with inline-style colored cells, an HTML-escaped label, and NES-themed styling. Includes HTML escaping for XSS prevention and hex color validation with #000000 fallback. No external dependencies or network requests.

nodes.name: src/utils/buildAvatarHTML.ts
nodes.summary: Node: file:src/utils/buildAvatarHTML.ts

properties.summary: Vitest test suite for htmlExport. Tests: Blob creation with text/html MIME type, URL.createObjectURL call, correct filename format (pixelforce-prefix-username.html), URL.revokeObjectURL cleanup, filename sanitization of special characters, and no-throw guarantee for valid input.

nodes.name: src/utils/htmlExport.test.ts
nodes.summary: Node: file:src/utils/htmlExport.test.ts

properties.summary: Exports a pixel avatar as a downloadable HTML file. Builds HTML via buildAvatarHTML, creates a Blob with text/html type, generates an object URL, and triggers download via a hidden anchor element. Sanitizes prefix and username for safe filenames.

nodes.name: src/utils/htmlExport.ts
nodes.summary: Node: file:src/utils/htmlExport.ts

properties.summary: Vitest test suite for exportAsPng. Tests: OffscreenCanvas creation with correct dimensions (n*16), convertToBlob call with image/png type, and no-throw guarantee for valid PixelMapResponse input.

nodes.name: src/utils/pngExport.test.ts
nodes.summary: Node: file:src/utils/pngExport.test.ts

properties.summary: Exports a pixel avatar as a downloadable PNG image. Uses OffscreenCanvas API (16x scale per cell) to render the pixel grid, converts to PNG blob, and triggers download via hidden anchor element. Sanitizes type and name for safe filenames. Failures are silently caught.

nodes.name: src/utils/pngExport.ts
nodes.summary: Node: file:src/utils/pngExport.ts

properties.summary: Exports a sanitize() function that lowercases a string and removes all non-alphanumeric characters except hyphens. Collapses multiple hyphens and trims leading/trailing hyphens. Used for generating safe filenames.

nodes.name: src/utils/sanitize.ts
nodes.summary: Node: file:src/utils/sanitize.ts

properties.summary: Vite client type reference directive. Provides TypeScript type declarations for Vite-specific features like import.meta.env.

nodes.name: src/vite-env.d.ts
nodes.summary: Node: file:src/vite-env.d.ts

properties.summary: Vite configuration. Enables React plugin and vite-plugin-singlefile for bundling into a single HTML file. Configures @ path alias pointing to ./src.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Node: file:vite.config.ts

properties.summary: Vitest test runner configuration. Sets jsdom as the test environment, enables globals, configures @ path alias matching Vite, and specifies ./src/test-setup.ts as the setup file.

nodes.name: vitest.config.ts
nodes.summary: Node: file:vitest.config.ts

properties.summary: Union type of supported LLM provider identifiers: 'anthropic' | 'openai' | 'openai-compatible' | 'deepseek'

nodes.name: LLMProviderType
nodes.summary: Node: file:type:LLMProviderType

properties.summary: Interface for provider configuration including type, apiUrl, and model.

nodes.name: ProviderConfig
nodes.summary: Node: file:type:ProviderConfig

properties.summary: Interface for predefined provider presets with type, label, apiUrl, and model fields.

nodes.name: ProviderPreset
nodes.summary: Node: file:type:ProviderPreset

properties.summary: Array of predefined provider presets for Anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-20250514), OpenAI (gpt-4o), OpenAI-Compatible (Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-Turbo via Together), and DeepSeek (deepseek-v4-flash).

nodes.name: PROVIDER_PRESETS
nodes.summary: Node: file:const:PROVIDER_PRESETS

properties.summary: Interface for LLM generation requests: prompt, apiUrl, model, and apiKey.

nodes.name: PixelGenRequest
nodes.summary: Node: file:type:PixelGenRequest

properties.summary: Interface for parsed pixel avatar response: palette (hex color array), grid (2D hex color array), and label (display name).

nodes.name: PixelMapResponse
nodes.summary: Node: file:type:PixelMapResponse

properties.summary: Interface for application errors with message, code string, and optional HTTP status.

nodes.name: AppError
nodes.summary: Node: file:type:AppError

properties.summary: Tagged union type for fallible operations: { ok: true, data: T } | { ok: false, error: E }. Universal return type across the codebase.

nodes.name: Result
nodes.summary: Node: file:type:Result

properties.summary: Interface contract for all LLM provider implementations. Defines a single async generate() method that takes a PixelGenRequest and returns a Result<PixelMapResponse>.

nodes.name: LLMProvider
nodes.summary: Node: file:interface:LLMProvider

properties.summary: Implements LLMProvider for DeepSeek API. Handles DeepSeek-specific features: truncation detection via finish_reason='length', default model deepseek-v4-flash, thinking disabled in request.

nodes.name: DeepSeekProvider
nodes.summary: Node: class:DeepSeekProvider

properties.summary: Implements LLMProvider for OpenAI API. Uses standard chat completions format. Handles API errors, empty responses, and network failures.

nodes.name: OpenAIProvider
nodes.summary: Node: class:OpenAIProvider

properties.summary: Implements LLMProvider for OpenAI-compatible APIs (e.g. Together AI, Groq). Default model is Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-Turbo. Handles API errors, empty responses, and network failures.

nodes.name: OpenAICompatibleProvider
nodes.summary: Node: class:OpenAICompatibleProvider

properties.summary: Central dispatch function that routes PixelGenRequest to the correct LLMProvider by type string. Lazy-initializes provider singletons. Returns INVALID_PROVIDER error for unknown types.

nodes.name: generate (llmClient)
nodes.summary: Node: function:generate (llmClient)

properties.summary: Parses a raw JSON string from an LLM into PixelMapResponse. Validates: non-empty palette of hex colors, non-empty square grid of hex colors, and non-empty string label. Returns Result<PixelMapResponse> with PARSE_ERROR on failure.

nodes.name: parsePixelMapResponse
nodes.summary: Node: function:parsePixelMapResponse

properties.summary: System prompt instructing the LLM to act as a pixel avatar generator. Enforces JSON output schema: palette, 16x16 hex color grid, and display label. Requires NES-inspired 64-color palette.

nodes.name: SYSTEM_PROMPT
nodes.summary: Node: file:const:SYSTEM_PROMPT

properties.summary: Builds a complete, self-contained HTML document for pixel avatar display. Accepts color grid, label, prefix, and username. Generates CSS grid layout with inline-style cells, NES-themed styling, and HTML-escaped content. No external dependencies.

nodes.name: buildAvatarHTML
nodes.summary: Node: function:buildAvatarHTML

properties.summary: Exports a pixel avatar as a downloadable HTML file. Builds HTML via buildAvatarHTML, creates text/html Blob, generates object URL, and triggers download. Sanitizes filename parts.

nodes.name: htmlExport
nodes.summary: Node: function:htmlExport

properties.summary: Exports a pixel avatar as a downloadable PNG file. Renders grid to OffscreenCanvas at 16x scale, converts to PNG Blob, and triggers download. Uses promise chain with silent failure catch.

nodes.name: exportAsPng
nodes.summary: Node: function:exportAsPng

properties.summary: Sanitizes a string for safe use in filenames: lowercases, removes non-alphanumeric chars except hyphens, collapses multiple hyphens, and trims leading/trailing hyphens.

nodes.name: sanitize
nodes.summary: Node: function:sanitize

properties.summary: Private helper that escapes HTML special characters (&, <, >, ", ') to prevent XSS in generated HTML output.

nodes.name: escapeHtml
nodes.summary: Node: function:escapeHtml

properties.summary: Private helper that validates a 6-digit hex color string (e.g. #ff0000) using regex.

nodes.name: isValidHex
nodes.summary: Node: function:isValidHex

layers.name: UI Layer
layers.description: React components, CSS modules, HTML entry point, and app root. Contains all presentation-layer code including the main App shell, control panel, viewer shell, theme toggle, API key input, provider select, avatar grid, download buttons, error/loading displays, and the Vite HTML entry point.

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Custom React hooks for state management and LLM provider service implementations. Hooks manage theme, API key, provider config, and pixel generation lifecycle. Services implement the Strategy pattern for LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenAI-Compatible, DeepSeek) with a central dispatch client, shared constants, response parsing, and the LLMProvider interface contract.

layers.name: Types Layer
layers.description: Core TypeScript type and interface definitions including LLMProviderType union, ProviderConfig, PixelGenRequest, PixelMapResponse, AppError, Result tagged union, and PROVIDER_PRESETS constant.

layers.name: Utility Layer
layers.description: Pure utility functions for self-contained HTML document building, client-side PNG export via OffscreenCanvas, HTML file export/download, filename sanitization, and HTML escaping/XSS prevention.

layers.name: Config Layer
layers.description: Build toolchain configuration files including Vite/Vitest config, TypeScript configs (tsconfig.json, tsconfig.app.json, tsconfig.node.json), package.json with scripts and dependencies, OpenCode config, sprint status YAML, and Vite environment type declarations.

layers.name: Test Layer
layers.description: Vitest test suites for UI components (ApiKeyInput, AvatarGrid, ControlPanel, DownloadButton, DownloadHtmlButton, ErrorDisplay, LoadingIndicator, ProviderSelect, ThemeToggle, ViewerShell), hooks (usePixelGeneration), and utility functions (buildAvatarHTML, htmlExport, pngExport). Includes test setup with @testing-library/jest-dom.

layers.name: Planning Layer
layers.description: Project documentation and planning artifacts including PRDs, requirement specs, implementation story specifications, epic breakdowns, UX design specifications, architecture decision records, and deferred work tracking.

tour.title: Project Overview & Planning
tour.description: Start with the README to understand the project's purpose, architecture, and setup. Then review the PRD, architecture decision doc, and UX specification to understand the design rationale and planning context before diving into code.

tour.title: Build Tooling & Configuration
tour.description: Examine the build pipeline and TypeScript configuration. Vite + vite-plugin-singlefile bundles the entire app into a single HTML file. Vitest provides the test runner with jsdom environment. The @ path alias maps to ./src.

tour.title: Application Entry Point & App Shell
tour.description: Follow the boot sequence: index.html loads the Vite module script, main.tsx mounts App in React StrictMode, and App.tsx composes all top-level components — ThemeToggle, ApiKeyInput, ProviderSelect, ControlPanel, and ViewerShell — wired to custom hooks.

tour.title: UI Components
tour.description: Explore the React component layer. ViewerShell implements a state machine (empty/loading/result/error). ControlPanel provides the prompt textarea, preset chips, and FORGE button. Supporting components include ApiKeyInput (masked password field), ProviderSelect (custom dropdown with keyboard nav), ThemeToggle, AvatarGrid (CSS grid renderer), LoadingIndicator (animated pixel square), ErrorDisplay (dismissible alert), and DownloadButton/DownloadHtmlButton for PNG/HTML export.

tour.title: Custom Hooks & State Management
tour.description: Review the four custom React hooks that manage application state: useTheme (dark/light toggle with localStorage persistence), useApiKey (API key in sessionStorage), useProviderConfig (LLM provider selection with auto-save), and usePixelGeneration (generation lifecycle — pixelMap, isGenerating, error handling, selectedType, reset).

tour.title: Core Types & LLM Provider Abstraction
tour.description: The type system lives in pixelmap.ts: LLMProviderType union, ProviderConfig, PixelGenRequest, PixelMapResponse, AppError, and the Result<T,E> tagged union. The LLMProvider interface in providers.ts defines the Strategy pattern contract. Four implementations follow the same Chat Completions pattern: AnthropicProvider, OpenAIProvider, OpenAICompatibleProvider, DeepSeekProvider. The llmClient.ts dispatcher routes requests by provider type. The parseResponse function validates LLM JSON output against the PixelMapResponse schema.

tour.title: Utilities: HTML & PNG Export
tour.description: The utility layer handles avatar export. buildAvatarHTML creates a self-contained HTML document (CSS grid, inline cell styles, HTML escaping for XSS prevention). htmlExport wraps it in a Blob download flow with filename sanitization. exportAsPng renders the pixel grid on OffscreenCanvas at 16x scale and triggers a PNG download. sanitize is a shared helper for safe filenames.

tour.title: Test Coverage & Implementation Artifacts
tour.description: Every module has a corresponding test file. Review test strategy patterns across components (screen queries, user events), hooks (renderHook, waitFor), services (MSW-style fetch mocking for 401/500/network errors), and utilities (HTML structure assertions). The implementation story specs detail the acceptance criteria that drove development.
